name: Halliburton Company

profile.description: Halliburton is one of the world's largest oilfield services companies, providing drilling, completion, production, and digital solutions across North America and international markets. Q1 2026 revenue was $5.4B (flat YoY) with 13% operating margin and EPS of $0.55. Drilling & Evaluation grew 4% while Completion & Production declined 3% on lower North American stimulation activity.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Flat revenue and margin compression from North American activity decline. AI Deployment (1/2): Digital oilfield solutions with AI-enhanced drilling optimization. Market Share (1/2): Global #2 oilfield services behind SLB [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Drilling & Evaluation growing 4%, digital platform expansion. R&D Intensity (0/1): Standard for oilfield services.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Providing energy solutions. Community Impact (0/2): No specific programs. Employee Retention (1/2): 46,000 employees with industry-standard retention. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Oilfield services — drilling, completions, cementing, wireline — are physical operations requiring heavy equipment, geological expertise, and on-site crews that AI cannot substitute [1]. AI enhances drilling efficiency but cannot replace the physical service delivery. AI Opportunity (0/1): No quantified AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: Halliburton operates as a cyclical oilfield services provider focused on operational efficiency and shareholder returns rather than a transformative purpose.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Providing energy solutions — functional purpose without distinctive vision [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): 46,000 employees with industry-standard stability.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Competitive focus on market share against SLB.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (0/2): Same oilfield services model for decades.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (0/2): Standard cyclical operations management [1].

investment_thesis: HAL at 13.4x forward P/E with 13% operating margins and PEG of 1.08 offers fair value for the #2 global oilfield services franchise [1]. International activity growth (D&E up 4%) partially offsets North American weakness.
thesis_continued: Revenue flat YoY with Completion & Production declining 3% on lower North American stimulation activity [1]. Profit margin of 6.9% is thin and cyclically vulnerable. Energy transition risks may structurally reduce long-term oil demand.
key_catalysts: Digital oilfield solutions driving margin expansion
key_risks: Oil price decline compressing customer spending
Energy transition reducing long-term fossil fuel demand
sources: Halliburton Q1 2026 Earnings Release — SEC Filing, April 2026
Yahoo Finance — HAL Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
